sections.heading: What it does
sections.body: Creative sidechain effects for dummies. A tempo-synced curve shaper: a beat-locked curve modulates volume (duck or boost) and/or a multimode filter's cutoff. Sync only — it locks to the host clock.

sections.heading: Transport-synced
sections.body: Tempo-sync only: PushNPull is bypassed when the transport is stopped. With no clock running, audio passes through dry/untouched; the effect kicks back in when you hit play.

sections.heading: Curve / Rate
sections.body: Pick one of 12 curve shapes; Rate sets the cycle rate (1/8, 1/4, 1/2, 1/1).

sections.heading: Volume / Filter
sections.body: Bipolar depths, one per target. 0% = Off; negative ducks, positive pumps/boosts. Run either or both.

sections.heading: Slope / Shift
sections.body: Slope sets how wide the active part of the curve is; Shift slides it in time for groove. Neither changes depth.

sections.heading: Attack
sections.body: Softens the onset — eases into the curve so the entry isn't an instant snap. Depth is preserved; 0% = instant.

sections.heading: Band Split
sections.body: Duck only the lows below Band Freq, leaving the highs untouched — invisible low-end ducking.
